title: trichelostylis autumnalis
side_effects: Trichelostylis autumnalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. Ingesting the plant's leaves or seeds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils or extracts can cause respiratory problems, such as asthma or bronchitis, in people with pre-existing conditions. Additionally, using Trichelostylis autumnalis for medicinal purposes without proper guidance can lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate underlying health issues.
